dual_degree.note: FIRST REPEAT ARTIST IN THE ENGINE. Ron Artis Jr and Ron Artis II are the same person -- 'II' and 'Jr' both designate the son of Ron Artis Sr. Degree 24 viewed through Soul/Jazz lens (Motown dynasty, Hawaiian culture, KEXP). Degree 39 viewed through Blues/Soul lens (blues guitar, self-released independence, PNW migration). Every CSV field except the underlying person has changed between the two entries.

location.origin: Haleiwa, North Shore, Oahu, Hawaii (same as degree 24). Born August 31, 1986. Son of Ron Artis Sr. (Motown session keyboards, Thriller) and Victoria Artis (vocalist, toured with Lena Horne). Second eldest of eleven children. Family relocated from mainland to Oahu when Ron II was approximately four years old.
location.formative: Same as degree 24: Ron Artis Family Band (Haleiwa, childhood through present); father's Motown instruction; Hawaiian kanikapila culture; multi-instrumentalism as cultural default. Additionally at degree 39: the blues festival touring circuit (Waterfront Blues Festival Portland 2019/2022, Telluride Blues & Brews, AmericanaFest Nashville 2024) as continuing blues education.
location.career: DEGREE 39 FOCUS -- Pacific Northwest touring circuit deepening: Neptune Theatre Seattle (October 2022, March 2023), The Rabbit Box Seattle (July 2025), Waterfront Blues Festival Portland (2019, 2022), The Reser Beaverton (December 2022), Portland Folk Festival (January 2023), Alberta Street Pub Portland (April 2025), Central Oregon (January 2026). Discography: What Music Means to Me (2016), Soul Street (2018), Purpose (2021), We Are Not Machines (2024, Grammy consideration via John Oates). All self-released. 15+ countries toured. Maui fire instrument donation December 2023 (200+ instruments, ABC GMA). 2026 summer tour booking active (Agence Resonances).

formed: Ron Artis II & The Truth (active); Ron Artis Family Band (childhood through present)
labels: Self-released (all four studio albums: What Music Means to Me 2016, Soul Street 2018, Purpose 2021, We Are Not Machines 2024). Note: degree 24 listed KEXP as media-institution label proxy; degree 39 lists self-released. Both are true -- the artist has always self-released; KEXP provides amplification without contractual relationship.

members.name: Ron Artis II / Ron Artis Jr
members.role: Lead vocals, guitar, piano, ukulele, bass, songwriter, bandleader
members.note: Dual-degree artist: degree 24 (Soul/Jazz, E=4) and degree 39 (Blues/Soul, E=5). Born August 31, 1986. Son of Motown session player Ron Artis Sr. Blues guitar comparisons to Hendrix and Gary Clark Jr. 'A voice from heaven, a cross between Eric Bibb and Al Jarreau' (Blues & Soul Magazine).

members.name: Stevon Artis
members.note: Brother. One of eleven Artis siblings.

members.name: Thunderstorm Artis
members.note: Brother. The Voice Season 18 finalist (2020). Duo shows at The Reser, Beaverton (December 2022).

associated_musicians.name: Booker T. Jones
associated_musicians.note: Degree 38. Documented collaborator at degree 24. Now geometrically adjacent (38-39). The Motown-Stax bridge -- Stax architect collaborating with Motown session player's son -- is now a structural adjacency in the engine.

associated_musicians.name: Robert Cray
associated_musicians.note: Degree 35. Blues entered the engine at degree 35 with Cray's stinging clean Stratocaster. Ron Artis Jr enters the blues zone at degree 39 through the Blues/Soul compound. Both share precision and emotional control.

associated_musicians.name: Jimi Hendrix
associated_musicians.note: Degree 36. Critics compare Ron Artis Jr's playing to Hendrix. Both have Maui connections: Hendrix's Rainbow Bridge concert (1970); Artis's Maui fire instrument donation (2023).

associated_musicians.name: Ayron Jones
associated_musicians.note: Degree 37. Both active on Seattle blues circuit. Both E=5. Ayron Jones = The Inheritor (Seattle-born). Ron Artis Jr = The Returning Wave (Hawaii-born, building Seattle presence).

associated_musicians.name: Allen Stone
associated_musicians.note: Degree 23. Both appeared in the Soul/Jazz zone together. Both independent artists. Different genre lenses at their respective appearances.

associated_musicians.name: Quincy Jones
associated_musicians.note: Degree 0. Jones produced Thriller; Ron Artis Sr. played keyboards on Thriller. Degrees 0, 24, and 39 share a literal album credit connection.

associated_musicians.name: Mick Fleetwood
associated_musicians.note: Hawaii music community connection. Documented at degree 24.

associated_musicians.name: Jack Johnson
associated_musicians.note: Ron Artis II opened for Jack Johnson's North American tour. Documented at degree 24.

associated_musicians.name: John Oates
associated_musicians.note: Hall & Oates. Submitted We Are Not Machines for Grammy consideration 2024.

associated_musicians.name: Joan Baez
associated_musicians.note: Folk-protest legend sharing stage with soul/blues singer from Hawaii. Documented at degree 24.

theory_nodes.concept: Dual-degree artist (the engine's first repeat artist -- a single person mapped at two different degrees, viewed through different genre lenses, at different energy levels, with different contextual framing; Ron Artis Jr at degree 39 is Ron Artis II at degree 24; every CSV field except the underlying person has changed; establishes that the engine's 360-degree model is genuinely multidimensional -- one biographical point maps to multiple musical-space positions)
examples: Ron Artis Jr (degree 39) / Ron Artis II (degree 24) -- FIRST dual-degree artist. Soul/Jazz (24) vs Blues/Soul (39). E=4 (24) vs E=5 (39). KEXP (24) vs self-released (39). Every dimension shifted.

Genre refraction: the same artist's music, heard through Soul/Jazz at degree 24, foregrounds Motown lineage and Hawaiian culture; heard through Blues/Soul at degree 39, foregrounds blues guitar and independent self-release. Both framings are true simultaneously.
Energy escalation: E=4 to E=5 across 15 degrees documents career growth. The dynasty energy at degree 24 has been supplemented by the artist's own migratory momentum at degree 39.

Structural implication: the engine's 360 degrees are not 360 unique people. They are 360 musical positions. A single person can occupy multiple positions. This changes the interpretation of the entire circle.
theory_nodes.description: The dual-degree artist is a structural category introduced at degree 39. It describes a single person who appears at two different points on the engine's 360-degree circle, viewed through different genre lenses with different energy levels and contextual framing. Ron Artis Jr/II is the first instance: degree 24 (Soul/Jazz, E=4, KEXP, The Island Export, The Invocation) and degree 39 (Blues/Soul, E=5, self-released, The Returning Wave, The Pacific Crossing). Every dimension changed between the two appearances. This establishes that the engine's 360-degree model is genuinely multidimensional: the degrees map musical positions, not people. One person can occupy multiple positions because one person can embody multiple musical traditions, genre identities, and energy states.

ancestors: All 38 prior single-degree artists -- each occupied exactly one position. Degree 39 breaks this assumption.
The genre compound system (Soul/Jazz at 18-19/23-24, Blues/Soul at 39) -- the mechanism that allows the same artist to appear under different genre framings.
The energy spectrum (E=1 through E=6) -- the mechanism that allows career growth to register as energy increase.
genealogy.relationship: The dual-degree artist is the most structurally significant finding since the engine began. It transforms the 360-degree model from a personnel roster to a multidimensional musical space. Track for additional repeat artists at future degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: The Returning Wave (fortieth artist-city pattern -- the repeat visitor whose accumulating returns to the Pacific Northwest deepen the gravitational bond between island origin and mainland destination; extends The Island Export from degree 24's static dual-geography to a dynamic migration pattern; the wave metaphor is Pacific: ocean waves return to the same shore repeatedly, each arrival reshaping the terrain; seven documented PNW performances 2019-2026)
examples: Ron Artis Jr (degree 39) -- seven documented PNW performances: Waterfront Blues Portland (2019, 2022), Neptune Theatre Seattle (2022, 2023), The Reser Beaverton (2022), Rabbit Box Seattle (2025), Central Oregon (2026). Pattern accelerating: one city (2019) to three+ (2022-2026).

Contrast with The Island Export (degree 24, same artist) -- static description of concurrent dual-geography. The Returning Wave adds temporal dimension: the accumulation of returns, the deepening relationship, the migration trajectory.

Contrast with The Touring Anchor (degree 38, Booker T. Jones) -- established artist making periodic appearances. The Returning Wave describes an artist actively migrating, building presence, expanding territory. The Touring Anchor is stable; the Returning Wave is accelerating.

The wave metaphor: Pacific waves return to the same shore repeatedly. Each arrival reshapes the sand. Each retreat carries something back to the ocean. Ron Artis Jr's returns to the PNW reshape the local music scene slightly; his retreats to Hawaii carry PNW blues influence back.
theory_nodes.description: The Returning Wave is the fortieth artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes an artist whose repeated returns to the Pacific Northwest deepen a gravitational relationship between island origin and mainland destination. The pattern extends The Island Export (degree 24, same artist) from static dual-geography to dynamic migration. The documented PNW performance pattern (2019-2026) shows acceleration: from Portland-only (2019) to Portland+Seattle+Beaverton (2022) to expanding into Central Oregon (2026). The wave metaphor is Pacific in character: ocean waves return to the same shore repeatedly, each reshaping the terrain. The Returning Wave connects to The Touring Anchor (degree 38) but differs in trajectory: the Touring Anchor is stable and established; the Returning Wave is accelerating and building.

ancestors: The Island Export (degree 24, Ron Artis II) -- concurrent island-and-mainland presence. The Returning Wave adds temporal dimension.
The Touring Anchor (degree 38, Booker T. Jones) -- non-residential gravitational connection through repeated performance. The Returning Wave is the accelerating version.
The Rural Draw (degree 23, Allen Stone) -- boomerang pattern (left origin, returned). The Returning Wave does not return permanently; it oscillates.
genealogy.relationship: Forty artist-city patterns across forty degrees. Seven PNW-to-global modes: Export (0), Incubator (30), Deep Root (35), Supernova (36), Inheritor (37), Touring Anchor (38), Returning Wave (39). The Returning Wave is the second non-residential pattern (after Touring Anchor). It is the first pattern defined by acceleration rather than stability.

theory_nodes.concept: The Pacific crossing (fortieth acoustic element -- the sound of a musical tradition carried across 2,680 miles of ocean, arriving on the mainland with island warmth intact but reshaped by the Pacific Northwest blues landscape; the first acoustic element defined by geographic displacement rather than by a specific instrumental technique; Hawaiian kanikapila warmth meeting PNW blues grit produces a hybrid timbral quality)
examples: Ron Artis Jr (degree 39) -- vocal warmth (Hawaiian soul lineage) over blues guitar structure (mainland blues tradition). Multi-instrumental fluidity (piano-guitar-ukulele switches mid-set) in blues festival context. Communal Hawaiian performance energy in PNW venue settings.

Contrast with the invocation (degree 24, same artist) -- the invocation was activation energy, summoning lineage into the room. The Pacific crossing is displacement energy, carrying tradition across geography.
Contrast with the congregational organ (degree 38, Booker T. Jones) -- specific instrumental timbre. The Pacific crossing is geographic-timbral, not instrument-specific.

The crossing is audible: warm vocal soul over blues chord progressions, mid-set instrument switches blues purists don't attempt, audience-inclusive performance style derived from kanikapila tradition.
theory_nodes.description: The Pacific crossing is the fortieth acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the timbral quality produced when a Hawaiian musical tradition (warm vocal soul, multi-instrumental fluidity, kanikapila communal energy) crosses the Pacific Ocean and lands in the Pacific Northwest blues circuit. Unlike all prior acoustic elements, which are defined by specific instrumental techniques or timbral qualities, the Pacific crossing is defined by geographic displacement -- the sound of a tradition reshaped by travel. Ron Artis Jr's performances at Portland's Waterfront Blues Festival and Seattle's Rabbit Box carry Hawaiian warmth into a blues context, producing a hybrid that neither pure mainland blues nor pure island music generates alone.

ancestors: The invocation (degree 24, Ron Artis II) -- same artist's prior acoustic element. The invocation summoned lineage; the Pacific crossing displaces tradition across geography.
Dynamic grit (degree 37, Ayron Jones) -- the most recent guitar-based acoustic element. The Pacific crossing returns to a guitar-inclusive mode after the congregational organ (degree 38).
The congregational organ (degree 38, Booker T. Jones) -- the most recent acoustic element. Keyboard-specific. The Pacific crossing is multi-instrumental and geographic.
genealogy.relationship: Forty acoustic elements across forty degrees. One per degree, no exceptions. The Pacific crossing is the first geographic-timbral element -- defined by displacement rather than technique. It marks the 40th element milestone. Track element 41 at degree 40.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the transoceanic bend (thirty-ninth deployment; the dynastic-soul blue note from degree 24, weathered by Pacific crossing and the blues festival circuit; rawer and more guitar-driven than the Motown-polished version at degree 24; the first blue note documented as a maturation of a prior deployment by the same artist; carries two oceans: warm Pacific origin and cold PNW destination)
examples: Ron Artis Jr (degree 39) -- the dynastic-soul blue note from degree 24, heard through blues lens. Blues festival circuit (Waterfront Blues, Telluride, AmericanaFest) demands emotional directness that strips away some Motown polish. The result is rawer, more guitar-driven.
Contrast with dynastic-soul (degree 24, same artist) -- warm, Motown-inherited, production-aware. The transoceanic bend retains the warmth but adds blues grit from PNW touring.
Contrast with gospel swell (degree 38, Booker T. Jones) -- organ-based, Leslie speaker Doppler. The transoceanic bend is guitar-and-voice based.
First blue note documented as evolution of a prior deployment by the same artist. Only possible because of the dual-degree structure.
theory_nodes.description: Thirty-ninth deployment of the blue note thread. The transoceanic bend is the dynastic-soul blue note from degree 24 (Motown-inherited, warm, production-aware), weathered by Pacific crossing and the blues festival circuit. The same artist's blue note, heard through the Blues/Soul lens rather than the Soul/Jazz lens, is rawer and more guitar-driven. Hawaiian warmth persists but is overlaid with PNW blues grit acquired through years of touring Waterfront Blues Festival, Telluride Blues & Brews, and AmericanaFest Nashville. This is the first blue note in the engine documented as a maturation of a prior deployment by the same artist -- a consequence of the dual-degree structure.

ancestors: Blue note -- dynastic-soul (degree 24, Ron Artis II): the same artist's prior blue note. Warm, Motown-inherited, production-aware. The transoceanic bend is its blues-weathered evolution.
Blue note -- the gospel swell (degree 38, Booker T. Jones): organ-based, Leslie speaker. The transoceanic bend returns to guitar-and-voice.
Blue note -- all thirty-eight prior deployments (degrees 0-38)
genealogy.relationship: Thirty-nine deployments across forty degrees. The transoceanic bend is the first evolutionary blue note -- documenting how a single artist's note-bending changes between two appearances in the engine. This is only possible because of the dual-degree structure. Track deployment 40 at degree 40.

theory_nodes.concept: Migratory tidal (eighth E=5 mode -- the energy of an artist whose career arc traces a geographic migration, building gravitational presence in a new territory while maintaining roots in the origin; bidirectional tidal force: Hawaii pulls home, PNW pulls toward the mainland circuit; the migration itself generates E=5 energy through accumulated presence)
examples: Ron Artis Jr (degree 39) -- E=5 migratory tidal. Energy increase from E=4 (degree 24) to E=5 (degree 39) documents career growth and migration momentum. Seven documented PNW performances (2019-2026). Grammy consideration (2024). Maui fire response (2023). International touring (15+ countries).
Contrast with dynastic-kinetic (degree 24, same artist, E=4) -- energy from family lineage. Migratory tidal energy comes from the artist's own career momentum, independent of family context.
Contrast with institutional tidal (degree 38, Booker T. Jones, E=5) -- energy through institution. Migratory tidal energy through geographic movement.
Contrast with breakthrough tidal (degree 35, Robert Cray, E=5) -- individual escape velocity. Migratory tidal is directional (island to mainland), not escape velocity.
Distribution after degree 39: 2:13:6:11:7:1. E=5 count rises to seven. Eight consecutive E=5+ degrees (32-39).
theory_nodes.description: Migratory tidal is the eighth E=5 mode in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the energy generated by an artist's geographic migration -- the sustained effort of building gravitational presence in a new territory while maintaining roots in the origin. Ron Artis Jr's E=5 at degree 39 (up from E=4 at degree 24) reflects the accumulated momentum of seven PNW performances across seven years, a Grammy-considered album, a Maui fire humanitarian response, and an international touring career. The tidal force is bidirectional: Hawaii pulls the artist home; the Pacific Northwest pulls the artist toward the mainland circuit. The migration itself -- the Pacific crossing, repeated and deepened over time -- is the energy source.

ancestors: Dynastic-kinetic (degree 24, Ron Artis II, E=4) -- same artist's prior energy mode. Family lineage energy. Migratory tidal adds the artist's own career momentum.
Institutional tidal (degree 38, Booker T. Jones, E=5) -- energy through institution. Migratory tidal energy through geography.
Successor tidal (degree 37, Ayron Jones, E=5) -- defined by predecessor relationship. Migratory tidal defined by geographic trajectory.
Personal tidal (degree 32, Luther Rabb, E=5) -- individual career energy. Migratory tidal adds directional geographic vector.
genealogy.relationship: Eight E=5 modes plus one E=6 mode across eight consecutive degrees (32-39). Progression: personal (32) -> scene (33) -> collective (34) -> breakthrough (35) -> transformative/E=6 (36) -> successor (37) -> institutional (38) -> migratory (39). Distribution: 2:13:6:11:7:1. Eight consecutive E=5+ degrees -- unprecedented.

seattle_connection: FIRST DUAL-DEGREE ARTIST -- Ron Artis Jr (degree 39) is Ron Artis II (degree 24). Same person, different genre lens (Blues/Soul vs Soul/Jazz), different energy (E=5 vs E=4). THE RETURNING WAVE as 40th artist-city pattern -- seven documented PNW performances (2019-2026): Neptune Theatre Seattle (2022, 2023), The Rabbit Box Seattle (2025), Waterfront Blues Festival Portland (2019, 2022), The Reser Beaverton (2022), Portland Folk Festival (2023), Central Oregon (2026). Accelerating migration from Hawaii to PNW corridor. THE PACIFIC CROSSING as 40th acoustic element -- Hawaiian kanikapila warmth meeting PNW blues grit, first geographic-timbral element. THE TRANSOCEANIC BEND as 39th blue note deployment -- dynastic-soul blue note weathered by blues festival circuit, first evolutionary blue note. MIGRATORY TIDAL as 8th E=5 mode -- energy from geographic migration, bidirectional Pacific tidal force. BLUES/SOUL as new genre compound -- bridges blues zone (35-37) and soul zone (24, 29-30, 38). EIGHTH CONSECUTIVE E=5+ (32-39). Distribution 2:13:6:11:7:1. SELF-RELEASED all four albums. Grammy consideration via John Oates for We Are Not Machines (2024). GENDER: 6:34 ratio (~15.0%). Male. MOTOWN-STAX ADJACENCY: Booker T. Jones (Stax, 38) adjacent to Ron Artis Jr (Motown lineage, 39).

theory_genealogy: Dual-degree artist (FIRST at degree 39; same person at degrees 24 and 39; establishes that engine's 360-degree model maps musical positions not people; one person can occupy multiple positions)
The Returning Wave (fortieth artist-city pattern; repeat visitor with accelerating returns; extends Island Export from static to dynamic; seven documented PNW performances 2019-2026)
The Pacific crossing (fortieth acoustic element; geographic-timbral element defined by displacement; Hawaiian warmth in PNW blues context; first non-technique-based acoustic element)
Blue note -- the transoceanic bend (thirty-ninth deployment; dynastic-soul blue note weathered by Pacific crossing; first evolutionary blue note documenting same artist's maturation)
Migratory tidal (eighth E=5 mode; energy from geographic migration; bidirectional tidal force between Hawaii and PNW)
engine_sequencing: E=5 CONTINUES at 39: Distribution 2:13:6:11:7:1. Migratory tidal as eighth E=5 mode. EIGHT consecutive E=5+ degrees (32-39). Track at degree 40.
GENRE Stage 20 at degree 39: Blues/Soul (new compound). Blues primary + soul secondary. Bridges blues zone (35-37) and soul zone (24, 29-30, 38). Track at degree 40.
DUAL-DEGREE ARTIST established at 39: Track for additional repeat artists. The 360-degree model maps positions, not people.
PACIFIC CROSSING as 40th acoustic element: Geographic-timbral. First non-technique element. Track element 41 at degree 40.
SELF-RELEASED continues: Most common label designation across 40 degrees. Track at degree 40.
EIGHT CONSECUTIVE E=5+ degrees (32-39): Unprecedented. The entire blues/soul/R&B zone operates at E=5+. Track at degree 40 -- will E=5 continue?

cultural_patterns: Artist-city taxonomy: FORTY named patterns (adding The Returning Wave at degree 39). Seven PNW-to-global modes: Export, Incubator, Deep Root, Supernova, Inheritor, Touring Anchor, Returning Wave. Classify every degree.
Gender ratio: 6:34 (women:men). Ron Artis Jr = male. ~15.0%. Track.
DUAL-DEGREE PRECEDENT: One person at two degrees is now possible. Track for additional instances.
BLUES/SOUL BRIDGE: Degree 39 connects blues zone (35-37) to soul zone (24, 29-30, 38) through a single dual-degree artist.
MOTOWN-STAX ADJACENCY: Booker T. Jones (Stax, 38) adjacent to Ron Artis Jr (Motown lineage, 39). The institutional rivals are now geometric neighbors.
HAWAII-TO-PNW MIGRATION documented as multi-year pattern (7 performances, 2019-2026). Track for deepening or plateau.
E=5 AS SUSTAINED MAXIMUM: Eight consecutive E=5+ degrees (32-39). Seven E=5 modes plus one E=6 mode. Track.
